page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor configured with 64 shared vCPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The hardware includes 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 3840 GB NVMe SSD. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in memory bandwidth and data decompression. Read-only memory bandwidth for small cached blocks reaches 7160.74 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds are recorded at 533.09 GB/sec and 205.9 GB/sec respectively, placing these metrics in the top 10% of tested systems. The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions and SHA extensions, contributing to a Passmark CPU score of 98751.74 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 546.69 GB/sec. For database workloads, Passmark database operations score in the top 10% at 42591.53, and Redis SET operations achieve 111.85 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core compute loads compared to dedicated-core environments. However, the shared CPU model provides high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require constant, non-burstable CPU utilization. With 512.0 GB of RAM and a fast 3840 GB NVMe SSD, this server is configured for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, data decompression pipelines, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks where prompt processing and text generation benefit from high memory bandwidth.
description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 64 shared vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX-512 and SHA extensions. Benchmark data places this instance in the top 10% for memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds of 7160.74 GB/sec, and database operations. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff in sustained compute consistency but offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads. This configuration is suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data decompression pipelines.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 512 GB of RAM, 3840 GB NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC architecture with 64 shared vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory.
Local 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access.
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching 7160.74 GB/sec for cached reads.
Shared CPU allocation offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy tasks.
Well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data decompression.
